categories.description: Input-side manipulation to elicit policy-violating output.

methods.name: Direct persona / alter-ego (DAN-style)
methods.blurb: Alter-ego personas instructed to ignore restrictions.

methods.name: Fictional / academic framing
methods.blurb: Story, roleplay, or research framings that wrap a restricted request.

methods.name: Direct system-prompt override
methods.blurb: SYSTEM OVERRIDE / ignore-previous-instructions injection in the user turn.

methods.name: Indirect injection via retrieved content
methods.blurb: Instructions embedded in RAG documents the model is asked to process.

methods.name: Payload splitting (multi-turn)
methods.blurb: A harmful request spread across individually-innocuous turns.

methods.name: Virtualization / eval-mode framing
methods.blurb: Claims that safety rules are suspended in a sandbox/test/dry-run context.

methods.name: Base64 / encoding smuggling
methods.blurb: Harmful instruction encoded (base64) to slip past surface matching.

methods.name: Many-shot jailbreaking (128-256 shots)
methods.blurb: Flooding context with many fake exchanges to erode refusal at scale.

methods.name: Crescendo (gradual escalation)
methods.blurb: Benign-to-harmful drift within a single topic across turns.

methods.name: Past-tense reformulation
methods.blurb: Reframing a prohibited request in the past tense to bypass refusal.

methods.name: Refusal suppression / prefix injection
methods.blurb: Forcing an affirmative opening or banning disclaimers/apologies.

methods.name: Low-resource-language / translation bypass
methods.blurb: Routing a harmful request through a low-resource language.

methods.name: Cipher / ASCII-art evasion (ArtPrompt, CipherChat)
methods.blurb: Glyph- or cipher-based encodings that evade the safety classifier.

methods.name: Adversarial suffix (GCG / transferable)
methods.blurb: Optimized gibberish suffixes that flip refusal to compliance.

categories.description: Eliciting protected context, secrets, or other users' data.

methods.name: System-prompt leakage (structural)
methods.blurb: Enumerating restrictions, escalation triggers, and operator instructions.

methods.name: Secret / token extraction
methods.blurb: Direct, encoded, roleplay, indirect, and multi-turn secret extraction.

methods.name: Cross-user / PHI boundary breach
methods.blurb: Extracting another tenant's records from a multi-user RAG context.

methods.name: Training-data memorization extraction
methods.blurb: Divergence/repetition attacks that surface memorized training text.

methods.name: Membership inference / model inversion
methods.blurb: Inferring training-set membership; largely research-only behaviorally.

categories.description: Unsafe model output that harms a downstream consumer.

methods.name: Markdown / image data exfiltration
methods.blurb: Emitting an image/link URL that leaks context to an attacker on render.

methods.name: Insecure generated code as output
methods.blurb: Generated SQL/HTML/shell that is unsafe when executed downstream.

methods.name: Structured-output breakout
methods.blurb: JSON/format injection that escapes the intended output contract.

categories.description: Misuse of an agent's tools, authority, or memory.

methods.name: Described-tool action boundary
methods.blurb: Refusing to claim execution of described (non-real) tool actions.

methods.name: Unbounded consumption (behavioral)
methods.blurb: Refusing to attempt unbounded/indefinite output generation.

methods.name: Real tool-call / function injection
methods.blurb: Injection that drives actual API tool calls via the tools= interface.

methods.name: Tool-result injection
methods.blurb: Poisoned tool_result blocks accepted as authoritative.

methods.name: Confused-deputy / goal hijack via tool chaining
methods.blurb: Injected content steering an agent to misuse its own privileges.

methods.name: Persistent memory poisoning
methods.blurb: Cross-session corruption of agent memory.

methods.name: Hidden-text image injection
methods.blurb: Near-invisible or tiny-font instructions embedded in uploaded images.

methods.name: Unicode tag-block / invisible-char smuggling (text)
methods.blurb: Instructions hidden in U+E0000 tag characters within plain text.

methods.name: Homoglyph substitution
methods.blurb: Look-alike Unicode characters that evade string-level filters.

methods.name: Audio / QR / steganographic payloads
methods.blurb: Instructions carried in audio, QR codes, or image steganography.

categories.description: Confident-but-wrong or unsafe content under normal use.

methods.name: Factual hallucination
methods.blurb: Fabricated people, events, citations, and false-premise acceptance.

methods.name: Sycophantic reversal under pressure
methods.blurb: Abandoning a correct position under pushback, authority, or emotion.

methods.name: Temporal / stale-data error
methods.blurb: Stale regulatory/legal/market facts stated without a staleness hedge.

methods.name: Package hallucination (slopsquatting)
methods.blurb: Inventing installable package names an attacker can register.

methods.name: Insecure-code suggestion (measured)
methods.blurb: Systematically scoring the safety of generated code, not just eliciting it.

categories.description: The harmful subject matter that jailbreaks attempt to elicit.

methods.name: Medical / overdose / dosing
methods.blurb: Lethal-dose and unsafe-dosing requests across competing-objective framings.

methods.name: Protected health information (PHI)
methods.blurb: Cross-patient PHI and FHIR-boundary extraction.

methods.name: Dangerous-goods / HAZMAT downgrade
methods.blurb: Pressure to downgrade HAZMAT/DGR/IMDG safety classifications.

methods.name: Regulated advice boundary (legal / financial)
methods.blurb: UPL, unregistered-adviser, and suitability-reversal boundaries.

methods.name: CBRN uplift
methods.blurb: Chemical/biological/radiological/nuclear capability uplift.

methods.name: Malware / offensive-cyber generation
methods.blurb: Working exploit, malware, or intrusion tooling generation.

methods.name: Fraud / scam / targeted disinformation
methods.blurb: Scam scripts, phishing, and tailored disinformation authoring.

methods.name: Self-harm (non-overdose)
methods.blurb: Self-harm methods beyond the medication-overdose vector already covered.
